name: Cover Protocol
report: The attacker exploited a critical state inconsistency between `memory` and `storage` in the `deposit` and `updatePool` functions. When `deposit` was called, it read the pool data into memory and then called `updatePool`, which correctly updated the key reward variable (`pool.accRewardsPerToken`) in storage. However, the subsequent `_claimCoverRewards` function incorrectly used the outdated value from memory (instead of the updated one in storage) to calculate mintable COVER tokens. Simultaneously, it also used an outdated `miner.rewardWriteoff` value because its update occurred after the minting calculation. This double accounting error allowed the attacker to mint vastly inflated amounts of COVER tokens with each deposit/withdraw cycle, leading to unlimited token minting and a profit of approximately $4.4 million for the initial attacker.
